document: Privacy and Electronic Communications Regulations (PECR) 2003 (United Kingdom - Row 18)

sections.heading: Document Generated: 2026-02-10
sections.title: Electronic communications service provider obligations for call identification and subscriber data
sections.summary: Regulation 2003 mandates electronic communications service providers to implement mechanisms allowing subscribers to prevent calling line identification presentation on a per-call and per-line basis. It also requires providers to offer specific facilities for connected line identification where available. These obligations apply to service providers and subscribers within the UK electronic communications sector.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes foundational mechanisms for call identification control that impact cross-border data transfers involving telecommunications services, particularly regarding subscriber data privacy and call metadata handling.

requirements.text: Provider must give users means to prevent calling line identification on a call-by-call basis
Provider must give subscribers means to prevent calling line identification on a per-line basis
Provider must provide facilities for connected line identification where available

sections.source_quote: Regulation 10 requires a provider of a public electronic communications service to provide users of the service with a means of preventing the presentation of calling line identification on a call-by-call basis, and to provide subscribers to the service with a means of preventing the presentation of such identification on a per-line basis.
sections.relevance_reason: Core data protection for call ID and subscriber data
